note_data.title: Atomic Structure
keyPoints: Atom is the smallest particle of an element that can take part in chemical reactions
Atom consists of three fundamental particles: electrons, protons and neutrons
Nucleus contains protons and neutrons while electrons revolve around the nucleus in orbits
Atomic number (Z) equals the number of protons in the nucleus of an atom
Mass number (A) equals the sum of protons and neutrons in the nucleus
Isotopes are atoms of the same element having same atomic number but different mass numbers
Electronic configuration shows the arrangement of electrons in different shells around the nucleus
Maximum number of electrons in any shell is given by 2n² formula where n is shell number
Valence shell is the outermost shell of an atom and valence electrons determine chemical properties

definitions.definition: Atom is the smallest particle of an element that can take part in chemical reactions.
The number of protons present in the nucleus of an atom is called atomic number.
The sum of the number of protons and neutrons present in the nucleus of an atom is called mass number.
Atoms of the same element having same atomic number but different mass numbers are called isotopes.
The arrangement of electrons in different shells around the nucleus is called electronic configuration.

importantQuestions.question: Define isotopes and give two examples.
importantQuestions.answer: Atoms of the same element having same atomic number but different mass numbers are called isotopes. Examples: Carbon-12 and Carbon-14, Hydrogen-1 and Hydrogen-2 (Deuterium).

importantQuestions.question: Describe the structure of atom. Also write electronic configuration of first 18 elements.
importantQuestions.answer: Atom consists of nucleus and electrons. Nucleus contains protons (positive charge) and neutrons (no charge) while electrons (negative charge) revolve in shells. Electronic configurations: H(1), He(2), Li(2,1), Be(2,2), B(2,3), C(2,4), N(2,5), O(2,6), F(2,7), Ne(2,8), Na(2,8,1), Mg(2,8,2), Al(2,8,3), Si(2,8,4), P(2,8,5), S(2,8,6), Cl(2,8,7), Ar(2,8,8).

mcqs.question: The maximum number of electrons in M shell is:
The atomic number of an element having electronic configuration (2,8,6) is:

commonMistakes: Students confuse mass number with atomic mass unit
Writing wrong electronic configuration by not following 2n² rule
Mixing up protons and neutrons in nucleus composition
Forgetting that isotopes have same chemical properties but different physical properties
Not writing complete symbol notation showing mass number and atomic number
examTips: This chapter usually has 2-3 MCQs and 1-2 short questions in every BSEK paper
Electronic configuration of first 18 elements must be memorized - appears every year
Always draw atomic structure diagram with proper labeling of nucleus, shells and particles
Isotope examples and their uses are frequently asked in short questions
Formula 2n² for maximum electrons in shells is must-know for numerical problems
formulas: Number of neutrons = Mass number - Atomic number
note_data.keyDiagram: Atomic structure diagram showing nucleus with protons and neutrons, and electrons in K, L, M shells with proper electron distribution. Must label all particles and show that nucleus is at center with electrons revolving in circular orbits.
